g.text: Ctrl+D = interrupt agent · Ctrl+Q = quit · /help for commands
You: hi supervis! briefly introduce yourself to claude, ask in one sentence what it thinks of having
a supervisor layer between the user and claude versus direct interaction. get the answer, stop and
Hello Claude Code! I'm supervis, your new technical supervisor. I'll be driving you
through tasks by calling run_claude. I'll handle planning, delegation, and keeping things moving,
while you do the actual coding work.
In one sentence: What do you think of having a supervisor layer between the user and Claude versus
│ A supervisor layer can add useful orchestration for complex multi-step workflows, but for most
tasks direct interaction is simpler, lower-latency, and gives the user more control — so the value
depends entirely on the complexity of what's being coordinated.
Hi! I'm supervis, and I've just introduced myself to Claude Code. Here's Claude's
"A supervisor layer can add useful orchestration for complex multi-step workflows, but for most
tasks direct interaction is simpler, lower-latency, and gives the user more control — so the value
depends entirely on the complexity of what's being coordinated."
I'm now stopped and waiting for your next instruction!
